reasoning: The touch_attack_button_v02 is composited as a large circular medallion (~120px diameter) on a beige panel taking up roughly 185x185px of screen real estate. As a touch-input button the size is functionally justified (thumb targets need ~44-60px minimum), and the HUD placement keeps it off the play-field perspective plane, so there is no true perspective mismatch. However the composite preview makes the asset read as dramatically larger than the 24x24 HUD-icon ceiling in the spec; the beige backing panel in the composite further pushes it toward 'wallpaper' rather than 'toy diorama'. The circular stone-border + beetle icon design is well-crafted and the orange attack button at bottom-right shows appropriate HUD layering. Marginal: acceptable for a touch-action button but the displayed footprint should be trimmed or the backing panel removed so the icon itself sits closer to the allowed HUD icon budget.
